rationale: The cited paper is directly about variety-seeking antecedents (nonconscious priming via shape arrays) and distinguishes variety from uniqueness, which closely matches the citing paper’s core construct and its positioning against prior trait/cognitive-activation work. The citing note explicitly says prior variety-seeking research focused on traits and cognitive activation, suggesting this 2008 JMRK paper is likely part of the conceptual baseline rather than an incidental mention. Given this strong topical and theoretical overlap, it warrants second-stage relation judgment.
